2025 Honest Review

The AI Tools Small Businesses
Are Actually Using in 2025

Most AI tool roundups are sponsored content dressed as advice. This one isn't. Here's what's actually worth paying for — and the implementation gap that determines whether any of it matters.

See the Tools → Claude for SMB
The Honest Landscape

Most AI tools are overhyped.
A few aren't.

The AI tool market in 2025 is noisy. There are hundreds of "AI-powered" products competing for your subscription. The reality: most of them are wrappers around GPT or Claude with a pretty UI and a markup. A small number are genuinely useful. The difference isn't the technology — it's whether the tool fits naturally into your existing workflow and whether your team actually uses it consistently.

~30%
of small businesses that subscribe to AI tools use them consistently after 3 months
5–8
the number of AI tools that actually move the needle for most B2B small businesses — out of hundreds marketed
80%
of available value from AI tools is left on the table without deliberate implementation and team training
The Tools Worth Using

What's actually delivering value
for small B2B businesses.

Verdict key: Buy It = worth the subscription, Conditional = worth it in specific situations, Skip It = rarely worth it for SMB.

AI Assistant · Core Infrastructure
Claude (Anthropic)
Buy It
$20/month (Pro) · $25/user/month (Teams)
The foundational AI tool for B2B small businesses. Claude Pro gives you access to Claude 3.5 Sonnet and Opus with Projects — which lets you build persistent, customized AI workspaces for specific use cases. Proposal writing, research synthesis, client communication, content creation, strategy work. The thing that makes Claude different from a generic chatbot is the Projects feature: upload your brand guidelines, SOPs, and client context once; use it in every conversation forever.
Best for: Any B2B company that does significant writing, research, or client deliverable work. That's most of them.
AI Research · Search & Synthesis
Perplexity AI
Buy It
$20/month (Pro)
Perplexity is AI-powered search done right. Where Google gives you links, Perplexity gives you answers — with citations. Exceptional for competitive research, industry analysis, market landscape work, and staying current on fast-moving topics. The Pro tier adds access to Claude and GPT-4 models for deeper analysis. Works best in combination with Claude: Perplexity for research and synthesis, Claude for writing and structured work product.
Best for: Sales teams, marketers, and consultants who spend significant time on research. Pays for itself fast.
Productivity · Knowledge Management
Notion AI
Conditional
$10/month add-on (on top of Notion subscription)
If your company runs on Notion — meeting notes, project docs, SOPs, knowledge base — Notion AI adds meaningful value. It can summarize long docs, generate first drafts within your existing workspace, and answer questions about your internal knowledge base. The catch: it only works if you're already in Notion. If you're not, start with Claude. If you are, the $10/month add-on is worth it.
Best for: Teams already using Notion as their primary workspace. Skip if you're not already there.
Meeting Intelligence · Async Communication
Otter.ai / Fireflies.ai
Buy It
$10–$20/month per user
AI meeting transcription and summarization. Every sales call, client meeting, and internal planning session gets auto-transcribed with action items extracted. Fireflies.ai is the better option for B2B sales teams (CRM integrations, deal intelligence). Otter.ai is simpler and works well for internal meetings and interviews. Either one pays for itself in the first week — you'll stop losing information from conversations.
Best for: Any team with regular client calls. Non-negotiable for sales-heavy organizations.
AI Image & Design · Marketing
Midjourney / DALL-E 3
Conditional
$10–$30/month
AI image generation has legitimate B2B applications — blog imagery, presentation visuals, concept illustrations, social content. But it's not a replacement for brand design work, and generic AI imagery is becoming recognizable and devaluing fast. Useful if you produce a lot of visual content and your brand doesn't rely on polished custom photography. Not useful if visual quality is core to your brand positioning.
Best for: Content-heavy businesses that need illustration and visual support at scale. Not a design system replacement.
Sales Intelligence · Lead Research
Apollo.io
Buy It
$49–$99/month
B2B contact data and sales intelligence with AI-powered features. Valuable for outbound prospecting — building targeted lists, enriching contacts, and sequencing outreach. The AI features (email personalization, intent signals) are useful but require deliberate setup to work. Use Claude alongside Apollo: Apollo finds and qualifies the contacts, Claude writes the personalized outreach at scale. That combination is genuinely powerful.
Best for: B2B companies with active outbound motion. Requires commitment to actually work the system.
Social & Content · Scheduling
Dedicated AI Content Tools (Jasper, Copy.ai, etc.)
Skip It
$39–$99/month
Most dedicated AI writing tools are Claude or GPT wrappers with a B2B content UI. They charge 2–5x what a direct Claude subscription costs. If you already have Claude Pro with Projects, you can build your own "content tool" in an afternoon with better customization than anything Jasper offers. We've yet to find a content-specific AI tool that justifies the premium over a well-configured Claude Project.
Instead: Build a Claude Project with your brand voice, content templates, and SEO guidelines. Same output, better control, fraction of the cost.
Evaluation Framework

How to evaluate any AI tool
before you pay for it.

1
Does it fit into how your team already works?
The best AI tool is the one people actually use. A tool that requires context-switching or a new workflow will be abandoned. Before subscribing, ask: does this tool live where my team already spends time, or does it require them to go somewhere new?
2
Is this a wrapper or a real product?
A lot of AI tools are GPT or Claude with a custom UI. That's not inherently bad, but you should know what you're paying for. Ask: what does this tool do that I can't do with a well-configured Claude Project? If the answer is "not much," skip it.
3
Can you measure the impact?
Set a baseline before you start. Time spent on the task, output quality, volume produced. Measure again at 30 and 60 days. If you can't show meaningful improvement, the tool isn't working — whether because of the tool or because of adoption.
4
What's the adoption plan?
Who is going to use this, in what workflow, how often, and what does success look like? If you can't answer these before buying, you're not ready to buy. The implementation plan matters more than the tool.
5
Is there a trial or refund window?
Most legitimate AI tools offer trials. If a tool doesn't have a trial and costs more than $50/month, be skeptical. You should be able to validate the value before committing to an annual plan.
The Implementation Gap

Having the tool is step one.
Getting it to work is the whole game.

Here's what we see constantly: a company subscribes to Claude, or Apollo, or five other AI tools. A few people use them sporadically. The subscription renews. Six months later, the CEO asks "are we actually getting value from all this AI stuff?" and nobody has a good answer.

The tools aren't the problem. The absence of deliberate implementation is. You need someone to design the workflows, configure the tools, train the team, and hold the system together until it becomes habitual. That's the gap between AI as a curiosity and AI as operating infrastructure.

That's what we do at Treetop.
